description: The 390 Adventure brings KTM's performance DNA to the ADV segment. It shares the 390 Duke's engine but adds long-travel suspension, a taller stance, and adventure-ready ergonomics. For riders who want a versatile bike that handles both highways and trails.
pros: Powerful engine with good off-road manners
Cornering ABS adds safety on all surfaces
Versatile for city, highway, and off-road
cons: Seat height is tall for shorter riders
Service costs are higher than smaller ADVs
Weight is noticeable on technical trails

avoid: Street tyres for serious off-road use
Cheap crash bars (they bend, not protect)
buying_advice.oem_vs_aftermarket: OEM accessories for the 390 Adventure offer perfect fitment and match the build quality. Aftermarket options are cheaper but check reviews carefully — some parts do not fit as well on premium bikes.
money_saving_tips: Buy during festive season sales (Diwali, Republic Day) for 15-30% discounts
Start with essentials, add accessories as you identify actual needs
Check Amazon, Flipkart, and local dealers — prices vary significantly
Invest in quality riding gear — it protects you across every ride
common_mistakes: Using road tyres for off-road — they cannot handle trails
Skipping crash protection — adventure bikes fall, it is part of the sport
Overloading for tours — weight affects handling significantly
Not reading the owner's manual for service intervals
